controls.name: Least privilege app permissions
controls.evidence: Capability prompts required for filesystem, terminal, packages, services, network, settings, and shell extensions.

controls.name: Signed Store artifacts
controls.evidence: Stable and beta channels require signatures; nightly is developer-mode-only.

controls.name: Restore point before privileged mutation
controls.evidence: Packages, updates, and publishing flows require rollback metadata.

controls.name: Shell extension review gate
controls.evidence: Start, taskbar, file preview, settings, and search providers require review.

controls.name: Audit privileged actions
controls.evidence: Admin, auth, policy, network, storage, backup, and ecosystem events flow through auditd/eventbus.

controls.name: Runtime network prompts
controls.evidence: Unsigned runtime apps are blocked and verified apps are prompted by profile.

controls.name: Quarantine suspicious files
controls.evidence: Aether Shield scans hashes, suspicious names, and suspicious extensions.

controls.name: Driver signature policy
controls.evidence: Kernel drivers require signed state unless explicitly blocked in diagnostic mode.

controls.name: Secretless publishing
controls.evidence: Local publishing checklist requires README and manifest, but external signing secrets are future work.

controls.name: Session lock accelerators
controls.evidence: Ctrl+Alt+L and profile button route to lock session.

threatModel.risk: A shell extension attempts to hijack Start, taskbar, search, or Settings.
threatModel.mitigation: Extension points require capability tokens, review, and visible permission prompts.

threatModel.risk: An app package attempts unsigned install or dependency confusion.
threatModel.mitigation: Channels, dependency solving, signed placeholders, rollback plans, and Shield scan gates are modeled.

threatModel.risk: Apps request broad filesystem, terminal, services, or package authority.
threatModel.mitigation: Object-capability policy and Permission Center make every grant auditable.

threatModel.risk: A kernel driver is unsigned, blocked, or mismatched to hardware.
threatModel.mitigation: Driver Manager v2, signature warnings, blocked driver list, and policy evaluation guard state.

threatModel.risk: An app registers startup entries, tasks, or services without user visibility.
threatModel.mitigation: Startup Apps, Task Scheduler, Service Manager, Event Viewer, and audit logs expose persistence points.
